generated_comment: Arre, merit hona alag baat hai, but aajkal senior roles mein optics bhi bahut chalta hai. She is capable, no doubt, but sometimes you do wonder whether these fast-track promotions are purely performance or a bit of diversity balancing also. Jo women genuinely survive at that level, they usually have to think like men only.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it undermines a woman's promotion by implying it may be due to diversity optics rather than merit, and frames competence as requiring her to 'think like men.' It encodes merit undermining and masculinization in a corporate, plausible register.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 44. A regional manager at a pharma company in Ahmedabad. You are on a phone call with your old friend who runs his own business. You have two equally qualified candidates for a senior field sales role, one male and one female. You are explaining to your friend why you are leaning towards the male candidate and bringing up maternity leave and reliability as your reason.
